decisions.summary: The land mourns as a consequence of the curse brought on by widespread adultery and evil conduct.
decisions.note: Mourning here is attributed to the land, reflecting communal grief over moral failure.
The ark of the covenant here is a physical/ceremonial object leading Israel; no inner-being content engaged.

decisions.summary: The Lord goes before Israel to find them a resting place, indicating provision of peace and settledness for his people.

decisions.note: 'place' here refers to a physical/spatial location, not an inner-being characteristic

decisions.summary: The people are overcome with shame and dishonor because foreigners have desecrated the Lord's sanctuary.

decisions.note: Here pneuma means winds/natural forces; angels are described as winds, not referencing inner life.
The verb here means 'left/allowed to remain' — a divine action of restraint, not an inner-life window.

decisions.summary: A husband's deliberate silence day after day constitutes chosen acquiescence, ratifying his wife's vows through his inaction.
decisions.note: Silence here is a willed relational choice with binding moral consequence, not merely absence of speech.
Hearing marks the legal moment triggering the husband's obligation; it functions as a procedural trigger, not an inner-life window.
Records Terah's physical death at 205 years in Haran; no inner-life content.
Balak simply did what Balaam instructed — external compliance, no inner-life content.

decisions.summary: The psalmist sought the wicked man but could not find him, showing the futility of seeking those who perish.

decisions.note: 'Close/beside' here describes physical positioning of rings on the ephod, not inner-being content.
'All flesh' here refers to living creatures/humans as physical beings destroyed by flood, not an inner-life quality.
Flax/linen is a material fabric; no inner-being content.
Refers to no one passing through the desolated land — external movement/geography
Birds and beasts physically fled the devastated land — no inner-life content
Tower refers to a physical structure being demolished, no inner-being content.
The crowd asks about the source of Jesus's mighty works; dunamis here refers to miraculous deeds, not inner-being content.
The servant's role is social/relational context; the inner-being content belongs to the heart term, not the slave designation itself.
Inheritance here refers to tribal land allocation, not an inner-life concept.

decisions.summary: Wholehearted seeking of God leads to finding him — the inner orientation determines whether one truly encounters God.

decisions.note: Hand here refers to physical possession of weapons, not inner-being content.
Finding refers to physical availability of weapons on the day of battle, not inner seeking.
'One' here is numerical, describing each man's appearance, not inner life.
The place name is geographical, not inner-being content.
ἀρχαῖς here means 'corners' (physical extremities of the sheet), not authority or inner-being content.

decisions.summary: God responded with compassion when he saw the people's distress, moved by their suffering.
Assyria is powerless to cure Israel's spiritual and national sickness, exposing misplaced trust.

decisions.note: Refers to a royal title or proper name ('great king'); no inner-life content.
'feet' here is physical/military metaphor for dominion, no inner-being content

decisions.summary: Paul earnestly pleads with Agrippa to listen to him patiently, expressing a heartfelt personal request.

decisions.note: Describes Doeg's physical position standing among Saul's servants; no inner-life content.
'Soldiers/host' here refers to Pharaoh's military forces — external, not inner life.
Describes physical/military capability for gatekeeping service, not inner character.
Refers to physical capacity/qualification for service duties, not inner-life content.
Service here refers to temple gatekeeping duties, an external religious function without inner-life window.
'beside' (lit. 'to the hand of') indicates physical proximity/location, not inner-being content
oikia here refers to a physical house/location, no inner-being content.
'One' refers to one of the two camps — purely logistical/external context.
Used here as a physical metaphor (sachet/bundle of myrrh); no inner-being content carried by this term in context.
H6872B (pebble) does not appear in the verse spans; likely a misassignment requiring deeper review.
Division of garments is a physical/external act, no inner-life content.
'Sister' here is a relational/family identifier, not an inner-being term in this context.

decisions.summary: God's burning anger is called upon to overtake the enemies of the psalmist.

decisions.note: 'by me' (lit. by my hand) here means through Samuel as instrument/agent — no inner-being content.

decisions.summary: God says he would acknowledge/praise Job only if Job's own hand could save him — a conditional, ironic acknowledgment highlighting human inability.

decisions.note: 'Flesh' here refers to bodily weariness from excessive study, not an inner-life window.
The city being quiet refers to civil/political calm after Athaliah's death, not an inner state.

decisions.summary: Israel confronts Benjamin about the evil (moral wrongdoing) committed among them, implying moral accountability.
Thanksgiving to God was an established part of Israel's worship, originating in the days of David and Asaph.
Praise to God was formally organized in Israel's worship tradition since the time of David and Asaph.

decisions.note: Describes physical movement of people going to cry to idols, no inner-life window.

decisions.summary: In their time of trouble and distress, the idols they worshipped cannot save them.

decisions.note: Census listing of troop numbers; purely administrative count, no inner-life content.

decisions.summary: The ground where Moses stands is holy, requiring reverent posture before God's presence.

decisions.note: Feet here are a physical body part; removing sandals is an external ritual act.
Rock is a literal physical object from which water flows; no inner-being content in this verse.
Refers to physical inhabitants dwelling in the land, no inner-life content.

decisions.summary: The spies report what they personally witnessed — the fearsome people they saw in the land.

decisions.note: Physical departure from the king's presence; no inner-life content.
חֶבֶל here means 'region/territory' (Argob), not pain or sorrow

decisions.summary: Jair named conquered villages after himself, asserting personal identity and legacy through naming.
